title: LATAM Prescription Digitization Gap
problem: Most prescriptions in LATAM are still paper-based. Patients lose prescriptions, pharmacists can't verify authenticity, no tracking of controlled substances. Fraudulent prescriptions common. No integration between prescribing doctors and pharmacies. COVID-19 accelerated digital prescriptions but adoption remains low (<20%).
solution: Digital prescription platform with electronic signing. Doctor prescribes via web/mobile app, patient receives SMS/WhatsApp link, presents at any pharmacy. Pharmacy verifies prescription digitally, dispenses medication, marks as fulfilled. Controlled substance tracking. Integration with national pharmacy networks.
technology: Digital signature/certificates, QR code generation, pharmacy POS integration, controlled substance database, mobile app
target_customer: Physicians, pharmacies, hospital systems, Ministry of Health (controlled substance monitoring)
traction_signals: COVID-19 forced regulatory changes allowing digital prescriptions
Proven models in Estonia, Sweden, Brazil pilots
regulatory_notes: Digital signature regulations, pharmacy licensing, controlled substance laws vary by country
varden_fit: HIGH FIT - Infrastructure middleware for prescriptions. Boring but essential. Government contracts possible for controlled substance tracking. Platform play connecting doctors + pharmacies.
notes: BOREALING: Electronic prescriptions are solved infrastructure in Europe. LATAM is 10 years behind. Boring middleware but massive scale (every patient visit).
